title: stapelia hirsuta var. vetula
intro: Stapelia hirsuta var. vetula, also known as the 'carrion flower', is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, known for its unique and foul-smelling blooms that mimic the appearance and odor of rotting flesh. The plant is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Stapelia hirsuta var. vetula include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Herbal preparations of the plant, often in the form of infusions or decoctions, are used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is necessary to fully understand its effectiveness and safety.
